title: Performance and Scalability
text: Performance and Scalability Uniface applications are enterprise applications that need to perform well under significant loads, and to scale well as loads increase and more users are added. The primary criterion of a usable application is that it performs its tasks (its application logic) as it was designed to do, and that it does so flawlessly. This in itself is a challenge for a software engineer. The most important secondary criterion is that the application should perform its tasks in an acceptable amount of time. An end-user of the application does not want to wait longer than necessary. The performance of an application is measured with respect to the time for one task to be executed by the application, that is, the time the user has to wait before proceeding. Related Links Component Memory Management At runtime, component instances require memory to handle the component definition and component data. This memory is not necessarily freed when the component instance is deleted. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
